input.background: As a pilot investigation for a survey of California county health departments, you want to select four counties at random from the list of counties in Table 2.1. Use the table of random digits (Table A) starting in row 33 to select your simple random sample.
input.data:
\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 2.1 California counties.}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|l|l|}
\hline 01 & Alameda & 21 & Marin & 41 & San Mateo \
\hline 02 & Alpine & 22 & Mariposa & 42 & Santa Barbara \
\hline 03 & Amador & 23 & Mendocino & 43 & Santa Clara \
\hline 04 & Butte & 24 & Merced & 44 & Santa Cruz \
\hline 05 & Calaveras & 25 & Modoc & 45 & Shasta \
\hline 06 & Colusa & 26 & Mono & 46 & Sierra \
\hline 07 & Contra Costa & 27 & Monterey & 47 & Siskiyou \
\hline 08 & Del Norte & 28 & Napa & 48 & Solano \
\hline 09 & El Dorado & 29 & Nevada & 49 & Sonoma \
\hline 10 & Fresno & 30 & Orange & 50 & Stanislaus \
\hline 11 & Glenn & 31 & Placer & 51 & Sutter \
\hline 12 & Humboldt & 32 & Plumas & 52 & Tehama \
\hline 13 & Imperial & 33 & Riverside & 53 & Trinity \
\hline 14 & Inyo & 34 & Sacramento & 54 & Tulare \
\hline 15 & Kern & 35 & San Benito & 55 & Tuolumne \
\hline 16 & Kings & 36 & San Bernardino & 56 & Ventura \
\hline 17 & Lake & 37 & San Diego & 57 & Yolo \
\hline 18 & Lassen & 38 & San Francisco & 58 & Yuba \
\hline 19 & Los Angeles & 39 & San Joaquin & & \
\hline 20 & Madera & 40 & San Luis Obispo & & \
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}
input.question: What are the first four random numbers selected from line 33 of Table A?

output.answer: The first four random numbers that apply are 18, 56, 16, and 38.
